assumptions: The question is assessed in the consolidated financial statements.
The contemplated hedge is an IFRS 9 hedge accounting relationship for foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies highly probable forecast transactions as eligible hedged items, including certain intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes recognised assets and liabilities as eligible hedged items, which is relevant where an intragroup dividend has resulted in a recognised receivable.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk that can be relevant when exchange differences are recognised in OCI under IAS 21.

approaches.label: IAS 21 foreign currency accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the baseline accounting for exchange differences on intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements, against which any hedge accounting overlay would be considered.
